entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Key Onboarding Release
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding continues to evolve with enhancements that streamline data management, improve user experience, and strengthen compliance. This blog highlights key updates across dependents handling, hiring status visibility, task management, notifications, document flows, and rehire controls—helping organizations deliver a more efficient and controlled onboarding journey.

Change In Availability of Dependents Information

Enabling the Country/Region-Specific Dependent Relationship Information setting in the latest People Profile removes the Dependents section from the SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding application.

Once this setting is enabled, the Dependents section isn't displayed for new hires and managers who use the Onboarding application. 

Instead, this information is collected through the Country/Region-Specific Dependent section in Employee Central after the Start Date of the new hire.


The Dependents section isn't displayed while performing the listed tasks mentioned earlier during the onboarding, internal hire, and offboarding workflows, as applicable.

Additionally, once the Country/Region-Specific Dependent Relationship Information setting is enabled, any dependent data included in the Onboarding process is ignored during the SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central hire process that is enabled using the latest People Profile's hire process. Therefore, the system doesn't save the Dependents data available in the Onboarding application, but automatically aligns with the Country/Region-Specific Dependent Information available in Employee Central.

Note:

Once the Country/Region-Specific Dependent Relationship Information setting is enabled, it can't be reverted.

How it is enabled:

Enable the Manage Employee Central Settings > Person, Employment, Worker Type > Country/Region-Specific Dependent Relationship Information setting.



Enhanced Hiring Status Indicator on Latest People Profile

Before MPH completion

HRs can only view the new hire's profile. Options to edit the data (Action buttons and Edit icons) are hidden. This ensures that no insert or edit actions are allowed from the profile, preventing conflicts and data sync errors.

The “All Actions” button,  Edit, and “Clock or History” icons are not displayed.

Hiring Indicator: Onboarding New Hire

 

After MPH:

After MPH, full edit access is restored, allowing HR and managers to edit and insert records as needed.

Hiring Indicator: Onboarding New Hire

 

For cancelled Onb records:

When the Onboarding process is cancelled, the new hire is now indicated as an “inactive user” without any action buttons available to manage the profile.

Hiring Indicator: Onboarding New Hire - Inactive

 

After start date:

Hiring Indicator: No indicator

 

Rehire cases:

For Rehire on Old Employment, the behavior of the Onboarding New Hire indicator and the supporting information text are the same as that for new hire.

For Rehire on New Employment, the Onboarding New Hire indicator and the supporting information text are not displayed.


New Task Management Page for Onboarding Tasks

You can now access the onboarding specific tasks from the Task Management page. Aim is reducing the dependency on the accessibility of the tasks only from the To-Do side panel.

The “Task Status” filter allows filtering the tasks by the following statuses relative to the current date:

All - Shows tasks in all statuses.

Overdue - shows tasks that are past their due date and still incomplete

Nearing Deadline - Shows tasks that are within 7 days of their due date but not yet past it.

On Track - Shows tasks that are more than 7 days before their due date.

 

The “Task Group” filter allows selecting a list of tasks that are grouped based on the following categories:

All

New Hire Activity

Compliance Forms

Data Review

Form I-9 and E-Verify

Custom Tasks

 

The Task Type filter lists an exhaustive list of onboarding tasks such as All, Assign a Buddy to New Hires, Complete e-Signature, and so on.

 

You can access the Task Management page from the Manage Tasks button on the Onboarding Dashboard:

 

To enable the Task Management page, select “Enable Task Management on Onboarding Dashboard” under Admin Center > Onboarding General Settings.

You can also create a custom quick link to the Task Management page on the latest home page.

 

Onboarding Notification Cards Available in Microsoft Teams

The SAP SuccessFactors app in Microsoft Teams now supports four Onboarding notifications cards, allowing users to receive important onboarding updates directly in Microsoft Teams (if New hires have their Microsoft Teams accounts provisioned by client’s organization).

Onboarding notifications that appear on the SAP SuccessFactors home page can also be delivered as notification cards in Microsoft Teams.

 

Currently, the following Onboarding notification cards are supported:

Welcome Aboard!: displays a welcome message from a manager or other onboarding participants.

Your Onboarding Checklist: prompts new hires to complete onboarding tasks.

Your Onboarding Guide: provides helpful onboarding information, such as preparation for Day 1, recommended links, key contacts, and onboarding meetings.

Complete E-Verify: Tentative Nonconfirmation: prompts new hires to respond to an E-Verify Tentative Nonconfirmation case result.

Compliance: notifies managers when it's their turn to take action on compliance tasks.

Configuration Requirements:

You've enabled the SAP SuccessFactors app for Microsoft Teams.

You've installed the latest SAP SuccessFactors app for users.

You have an Onboarding license.

Users are assigned as new hires in the SAP SuccessFactors system.

New hires have their Microsoft Teams accounts provisioned by your organization.

You've enabled these options in Admin Center > Work Tech Configuration > Notification Cards: Welcome Aboard!, Your Onboarding Checklist, Your Onboarding Guide, and Compliance

Restart e-Signature Step for Document Flows

As an onboarding participant, you can now restart only the Complete e-Signature step when you make changes to the new-hire data. This ensures that the updated information is reflected accurately without restarting the entire onboarding process.



Selecting the Restart button opens a Restart Task window that displays the documents attached in the selected document flow. You can enter the reason for the restart and confirm the action.

Once the restart button is initiated, the system regenerates the documents with the updated data and the task appears on the onboarding checklist clearly indicating the restart.

 

Multiple Document Flows

In the case of multiple document flows as defined in the Process Variant Manager (PVM), you can initiate the Restart action for a specific document flow as required.

Document Flow 1:

 

Document flow 2:

The list of documents for each flow as displayed in the Restart Task window helps you identify and distinguish the specific document flow you want to restart.

This restart functionality applies to onboarding document flows in various states, including in-progress, completed, and skipped scenarios.

A document flow is marked as Skipped under two scenarios:

  • When the assignment rules evaluate to conditions where the required form set does not need any action, such as forms that are informational only and do not require signatures.
  • When none of the rule conditions are met and no forms are assigned, indicating that no action is required from the new hire or onboarding participants.

You may need to restart a skipped document flow when incorrect or incomplete data causes the rule evaluation to skip assigning forms. By correcting the data and restarting the flow, the system can re‑evaluate the rules and assign the correct forms to the new hire.

In case of skipped Complete e-Signature task, the Restart Task window doesn't display the documents attached in the document flow. Once restart is initiated, the relevant documents are attached to the document flow based on the updated data and rule evaluations, if applicable.


Target Population Enforcement for Rehire Inactive Employee

You can now enforce target population restrictions for Rehire Inactive Employee (by match in New Hire) and Rehire Inactive Employee with New Employment (by match in New Hire) permissions in Onboarding.

When enabled, the system checks the rehire coordinator’s target population before allowing them to accept a rehire match, preventing unauthorized access to employee data.

Administrators can enable the new settings "Enable target population for rehire on old employment" and "Enable target population for rehire on new employment" in Manage Data.

Conclusion:

These enhancements to SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding reflect a strong focus on data accuracy, compliance, and user experience. By centralizing dependent information, improving hiring status visibility, expanding task and notification access, enabling flexible document restarts, and strengthening rehire controls, organizations can achieve a more streamlined and governed onboarding process. Adopting these updates will help HR teams reduce errors, improve transparency, and deliver a smoother onboarding experience for all stakeholders.

entry.title: Re: Employee's email address are not updated in the emplo...
entry.content:
Hello @vk36

Thank you for the clarification.

The blocker issue is no longer the employee master update itself, but the source that SAP CPQ is using for the email. Workforce replication (See KBA 2905918 - FAQs: Replicate Basic Master Data for Workforce from External HR Systems - SAP for Me for more insights) and the synchronisation into the Business Partner model are separated steps, and the workforce BP model separates employee and employment related data instead of exposing everything as one flat BP communication record. Because of that, when you maintain an email through Manage Workforce, you should not automatically assume that it will appear in the standard BP communication area that external integrations may read.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/1e57867e642c482a953f925f21546373.html?locale=en-US

So, if CPQ is currently reading the usual BP email field, then that explains your issue. Which means it is reading the wrong source for employee email.

 

I suggest you verify which object or API your CPQ integration is consuming today. If the integration is based on standard BP communication field, then this is not something you resolve by manually correcting BP. Instead, you should adjust the integration so that CPQ reads the workforce-maintained email source, or the middleware should map that workforce email into the field CPQ expects.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0bebd08dffca45afa67b1f751199afd0/9b79da541f294bc6a06bbbb5c987cc41.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/1db61fce01a04562bca8c2881f2f1d0c.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0bebd08dffca45afa67b1f751199afd0/f1b5b28b2ecb40af9e1bf52ead527539.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: how to develop an API Provider, API Proxy, Product, and Application in SAP API Management
entry.content:
This content is a stepwise instruction on how to develop an API Provider, API Proxy, Product, and Application in SAP API Management. It will provide users with insights on how to safely expose and operate APIs with OAuth and basic authentication schemes. You will get to know how to set up connections, create proxies and make APIs available as products. Also, the blog discusses how to build and maintain apps in the Developer Hub. The guide is aimed at SAP consultants and developers as well as beginners who deal with API Management.

 

Create API Provider ,API Proxy,Product & Application

Creation of API Provider-:

1. Under Config Section In API’s Navigate to API Provider Sub-Section and Click on Create 

2. In Overview tab enter the description 

3. In the Connection tab Enter the host details,Port Number & Check on Use SSL

4. In Catalog tab keep the default setting and click onSave.

5. Click on Test Connection IT should ping 200 status code now you have setup your API Provider.

 

Creation of API Proxy-: 1. Main Proxy (token verification & basic auth)
  2. OAuth Proxy (token generation proxy)

We will proceed with OAuth proxy first to create the Bearer token.

1. Navigate to API Proxy sub-tab and click on create as shown below. 

2. Select URL fill the details as shown below & click on create to generate the api proxy.

3. After Clicking on Create an api proxy dialog box will open navigate to proxy end points and fill the details as shown & click on save.

4. Click on Policies and navigate to the Policy Editor.

5. You will see two main sections
Proxy Endpoint: Handles client-side logic (e.g., OAuth, API key validation)
Target Endpoint: Handles backend-side logic (e.g., Basic Auth)
We will create policies on proxy end point.

6. Click on PreFlow under ProxyEndpoint, then add the following policies in order in request and response pipeline :

Request-pipeline policies

JS-Policy

1. On the right pane of the window click on edit and under extension policies group click on javascript policies as shown & click on add.

Now Click on the policy and paste the below xml code.

<!-- this policy allows us to execute java script code during execution of an API Proxy -->

<Javascript async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" timeLimit="200" xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>

 

    <!-- contains the name of the main code file -->

    <ResourceURL>jsc://ExtractParam.js</ResourceURL>

</Javascript>

Now we have to create the script for this JS policy
On the left pane of the window in scripts section click on + Icon fill the details as shown.

After Clicking on Add Button Paste this Script.

var gr_type_header = context.getVariable("request.header.grant_type");

var gr_type_queryParam = context.getVariable("request.queryparam.grant_type");

var gr_type;

 

if(gr_type_queryParam)

{

    gr_type = gr_type_queryParam;

}

else if (gr_type_header)

{

    gr_type = gr_type_header;

}

else

{

    gr_type = "";

}

 

context.setVariable("request.queryparam.grant_type", gr_type);

TargetEndpoint pre-flow policies

Request-pipeline policies

1. KVM Operations -:follow similar steps to add the policy.

Name -: KVM-GetParams

XML Code -:
<KeyValueMapOperations mapIdentifier="TestBasicAuthCreds" async="true" continueOnError="true" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">

<Get assignTo="private.username" index='1'>

<Key>

<Parameter>clientid</Parameter>

</Key>

</Get>

<Get assignTo="private.password">

<Key>

<Parameter>clientsecret</Parameter>

</Key>

</Get>

 

 

<Scope>environment</Scope>

</KeyValueMapOperations>

 

KVM

a.Duplicate the tab & navigate to config section under Apis click on Key Value Maps and hit the create button.

 

b. Fill the details as shown add the respective key’s encrypt the kvm and save it.

On the right pane of window click on update and navigate to proxy main window and click on save & deploy the proxy as shown.

Product & Application Creation

Publishing the product

1. On left pane of window navigate to engage section in product hit the create icon.

2. In create product dialogue box fill the section as shown and add the respective proxies

  1. Fill the mandatory fields in overview section as shown

    b. Navigate to api’s section and add the proxies and hit the publish button.

    Now the product is published and proxies are bundled together next we will create application in developer hub.

Developer Hub

Creating application

Prerequisites-: AuthGroup.API.ApplicationDeveloper
role must be assign to user sub-account if not assign it in sub-account under security section.


1. On the top right pane of window click on Explore Our Ecosystem dots and navigate to developer hub as shown.

2. In developer hub click on register button as shown.

3. Fill the details select select country/region as India.

4. Click on admin center navigate to users.

5. Move to Registered user section and assign developer role to your self by clicking on action.

6. Check box the developer and save it.

7. In Developer Hub Navigate to my workspace tab and hit the create button.

3. Follow the steps as shown.

4. After clicking on save button an application window will open which have client credentials copies 

the key and secret

 

The end result of this process is operational API set-up within the SAP API Management with an API Provider set to connect to the backend system.The API is packaged and released as a Product, and thus can be consumed.

entry.title: Re: Error in clearing reprocess bank statements
entry.content:
SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity is responsible for the secure transmission of bank statements into the SAP system. The error “999 line items reached” occurs during bank statement reprocessing and clearing inside SAP S/4HANA, which is part of the ERP application logic. As this step happens after the bank statements have already been delivered successfully, the issue typically needs to be analyzed within S/4HANA bank statement processing or clearing configuration, rather than on the MBC side.


SAP Help Portal:
Processing Bank Statements - SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b51655f09e1144828aeafec20470b9c8/896417031c6e43c8be4d2c87f44a82a7.html
Manage Bank Statements - SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/3cb1182b4a184bdd93f8d62e3f1f0741/e74a1f567afbdf21e10000000a4450e5.html


Thank you,
Mahendra C
MBC Team

entry.title: Re: Unable to connect SAP Content Server to S/4 Hana 2023
entry.content: Use <SYSID> instead of SID for transport through your system landscape

entry.title: Re: How do I access the Manage Bank Messages?
entry.content:
Manage Bank Messages is an SAP S/4HANA Fiori app used to monitor inbound and outbound bank messages exchanged with banks via SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity (MBC).

🔹 How to access the app

There is no separate external link or URL for this app.

To access it:

  1. Log in to your SAP S/4HANA system
  2. Open the SAP Fiori Launchpad
  3. Search for the app “Manage Bank Messages”
  4. Open the app to view messages sent to and received from banks via SAP MBC

🔹 Authorization note

If you do not see the app in the Fiori Launchpad, please ensure that:

  • Bank Communication Management is configured
  • The required business roles for bank communication are assigned to your user

🔹 SAP Help Portal reference : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b8e68a90bfff4ff0906d5db5342bdece/df2999dc360a496aa9ba30ad0496cc04.html

Thank you 
Mahendra C
MBC Team
